openmeter/portal/adapter.purpose: JWT-backed implementation of portal.Service that issues and validates short-lived HS256 tokens scoped to a namespace, subject, and optional meter slug allowlist. No database — tokens are stateless JWTs; ListTokens and InvalidateToken are intentionally unimplemented (return GenericNotImplementedError).

patterns.name: Config-validated constructor
patterns.description: New(Config) validates all fields via Config.Validate() before constructing the adapter; return (portal.Service, error) — never panic on bad config.
patterns.example: func New(config Config) (portal.Service, error) { if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err } ... }

patterns.name: Interface compliance assertion
patterns.description: var _ portal.Service = (*adapter)(nil) at package level — compile-time proof the struct satisfies the interface.

patterns.name: Noop for disabled/unimplemented methods
patterns.description: Methods not yet backed by persistent storage return models.NewGenericNotImplementedError; noop.go provides a full no-op implementation for test/disabled contexts via NewNoop().

patterns.name: JWT claims struct embeds jwt.RegisteredClaims
patterns.description: JTWPortalTokenClaims embeds jwt.RegisteredClaims and adds domain fields (Namespace, Id, AllowedMeterSlugs). Parse with ParseWithClaims into this type, not into map[string]interface{}.
patterns.example: type JTWPortalTokenClaims struct { jwt.RegisteredClaims; Namespace string; AllowedMeterSlugs []string }

patterns.name: Strict JWT parse options
patterns.description: Validate uses WithStrictDecoding, WithExpirationRequired, WithIssuer, and WithValidMethods — never relax these constraints when extending validation.

key_file_guides.role: Config struct, Validate(), New() constructor, adapter struct definition
key_file_guides.watch_for: Secret must be []byte internally; Config.Secret is string — conversion happens in New(). Expire=0 is rejected.

key_file_guides.role: CreateToken (JWT sign) and Validate (JWT parse + claims extraction); ListTokens and InvalidateToken stub out with NotImplemented
key_file_guides.watch_for: Token field is intentionally NOT populated in toAPIPortalToken mapping — the raw token string is only exposed in CreateToken response. Adding it elsewhere is a security risk.

key_file_guides.role: Full no-op portal.Service for tests and disabled-portal wiring
key_file_guides.watch_for: All methods must return models.NewGenericNotImplementedError, not nil error — callers distinguish 'not implemented' from success.

anti_patterns: Storing token state in a database here — this adapter is intentionally stateless; persistence belongs in a future DB-backed adapter
Relaxing JWT validation options (removing WithExpirationRequired, WithStrictDecoding, etc.)
Populating the Token field inside toAPIPortalToken — token string must only appear in CreateToken response
Calling New() without checking the returned error
Adding business logic to the noop adapter — it must remain a pure stub

decisions.decision: Stateless JWT tokens with no DB persistence for ListTokens/InvalidateToken
decisions.rationale: Short-lived portal tokens for metering access do not require revocation in the current design; unimplemented methods return NotImplemented to make gaps explicit rather than silently succeeding.

decisions.decision: HS256 with a shared secret rather than asymmetric signing
decisions.rationale: Portal tokens are validated by the same server that issues them, so symmetric signing is sufficient and avoids key distribution complexity.

code_examples.scenario: Creating and validating a portal token

openmeter/portal/authenticator.purpose: Chi middleware that authenticates HTTP requests against the v1 OpenAPI security requirements, dispatching to verifyPortalToken for PortalTokenAuth-scoped routes and injecting the validated subject into context.

patterns.name: OpenAPI-driven security dispatch
patterns.description: getSecurityRequirements reads per-operation security from the swagger spec (falling back to global); validateSecurityRequirements iterates alternatives and returns on first success — mirrors OpenAPI OR semantics.
patterns.example: for _, sr := range securityRequirements { r, err = a.validateSecurityRequirement(sr, w, r); if err == nil { return r, nil } }

patterns.name: Subject injected via typed context key
patterns.description: Authenticated subject is stored under AuthenticatorSubjectSessionKey (typed AuthenticatorContextKey string). Retrieve with GetAuthenticatedSubject(ctx) — never use raw string key.

patterns.name: Security scheme name matched by prefix split
patterns.description: getAuthenticatorFunc splits api.PortalTokenAuthScopes on '.' and matches by first segment — adding a new auth scheme requires a new case here.

patterns.name: Nil security requirement means unauthenticated route
patterns.description: If getSecurityRequirements returns nil (no matching route/operation), the middleware calls next directly without authentication.
patterns.example: if sr == nil { next.ServeHTTP(w, r); return }

key_file_guides.role: Entire authenticator: struct, constructor, Chi middleware factory, portal token verification, OpenAPI security resolution
key_file_guides.watch_for: verifyPortalToken extracts meterSlug from chi.URLParam — if the route param name changes this check silently stops enforcing slug allowlists. AllowedMeterSlugs check uses slices.Contains; empty slice means all slugs allowed.

anti_patterns: Adding new security schemes by hardcoding names as plain strings instead of using api.* constants split on '.'
Calling portal.Validate outside verifyPortalToken — token validation must flow through the middleware, not ad hoc
Skipping the AllowedMeterSlugs check when meterSlug is empty or route has no slug param
Returning 200 or 500 for auth failures — unauthorized must always be 401 via models.NewStatusProblem

decisions.decision: Security requirements read from swagger spec at request time rather than compiled into route handlers
decisions.rationale: Keeps auth policy co-located with the OpenAPI spec; new endpoints automatically inherit security without changing middleware code.

openmeter/portal/httphandler.purpose: HTTP handler package (package httpdriver) for portal token endpoints — implements the httptransport.Handler pattern for CreateToken, ListTokens, and InvalidateToken, delegating to portal.Service and meter.Service for validation.

patterns.name: Composite Handler interface
patterns.description: Top-level Handler embeds sub-interfaces (TokenHandler); each operation returns a typed handler alias. var _ Handler = (*handler)(nil) enforces compile-time compliance.
patterns.example:
type Handler interface { TokenHandler }
type TokenHandler interface { CreateToken() CreateTokenHandler; ListTokens() ListTokensHandler; InvalidateToken() InvalidateTokenHandler }

patterns.name: httptransport.NewHandler / NewHandlerWithArgs
patterns.description: Each endpoint is a closure pair: (decoder func) + (operation func) + ResponseEncoder + options. Params-bearing endpoints use NewHandlerWithArgs; param-less use NewHandler.

patterns.name: Namespace resolved via namespaceDecoder
patterns.description: resolveNamespace(ctx) wraps namespaceDecoder.GetNamespace; returns HTTP 500 on failure. Always call this at the start of every decoder func.
patterns.example:
ns, err := h.resolveNamespace(ctx)
if err != nil { return ..., err }

patterns.name: Domain input type as request type alias
patterns.description: Request types reuse domain input structs directly (CreateTokenRequest = portal.CreateTokenInput) rather than duplicating fields.

patterns.name: Token field added post-mapping in CreateToken operation
patterns.description: toAPIPortalToken deliberately omits the raw token string; CreateToken operation manually sets portalToken.Token = token.Token after calling the mapping helper.

patterns.name: WithOperationName option on every handler
patterns.description: httptransport.AppendOptions appends WithOperationName (e.g. "createPortalToken") to h.options for every handler — required for OTel span naming.

key_file_guides.role: Handler/TokenHandler interfaces, handler struct, New() constructor, resolveNamespace helper
key_file_guides.watch_for: New() takes variadic HandlerOption — callers pass error handler and tracer options here; forgetting them silences errors.

key_file_guides.role: All three endpoint implementations: ListTokens, CreateToken, InvalidateToken
key_file_guides.watch_for: CreateToken validates AllowedMeterSlugs against live meter list — this is an operation-level side call to meterService.ListMeters, not a decode-phase check. InvalidateToken returns 204 (EmptyResponseEncoder), not 200.

key_file_guides.role: toAPIPortalToken maps portal.PortalToken to api.PortalToken; sets Expired flag if ExpiresAt is in the past
key_file_guides.watch_for: Token field is intentionally NOT mapped here — always set it explicitly in the create handler after calling toAPIPortalToken.

anti_patterns: Returning the raw JWT token string via toAPIPortalToken — it must be set manually only in CreateToken
Adding database calls directly in handlers — delegate to portal.Service or meter.Service
Omitting WithOperationName from AppendOptions — breaks OTel tracing span names
Using context.Background() instead of the request context in decoder or operation closures

decisions.decision: Meter slug validation in CreateToken operation rather than inside portal.Service
decisions.rationale: portal.Service is JWT-only and has no knowledge of meters; cross-domain validation belongs in the HTTP handler layer which has access to both services.

decisions.decision: Request type aliases to domain input structs
decisions.rationale: Avoids duplicating field definitions; keeps the handler thin and the domain type as the single source of truth for input shape.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new portal endpoint following the existing pattern
